message:
test(fuzz): add symbol envelope nonce/AAD layout fuzz target (resolves gcqmu)

derive_nonce12, derive_nonce24, build_symbol_aad (symbol_envelope.rs:138-
193) are pure byte-layout primitives feeding ChaCha20-Poly1305 /
XChaCha20-Poly1305 AEAD. A regression in any field's byte position would
either:
- produce nonce reuse under the same key (catastrophic for ChaCha20)
- produce AAD mismatch and silent decryption failures

Existing symbol_envelope_decrypt round-trips through these primitives
but doesn't probe their layout invariants directly.

Six properties:
- determinism on all three primitives
- bit-level injectivity on nonce12 (frame_seq, esi)
- bit-level injectivity on nonce24 (sender_instance_id, frame_seq, esi)
- AAD per-field binding ×6 with raw-changed guard so we don't false-
  positive on no-op mutations
- stable lengths: nonce12=12, nonce24=24, AAD=86
- nonce24 zero-padding at bytes 20-23

Once-gated regression anchors verify exact byte positions per the
documented layouts:
- (a) derive_nonce12(0x0807060504030201, 0x12111009) yields exact LE
  bytes [0..8) frame_seq, [8..12) esi
- (b) derive_nonce24 with known inputs yields exact 24 bytes including
  bytes 20-23 = 0
- (c) build_symbol_aad with known SymbolContext: object_id at [0..32),
  esi LE at [32..36), k LE at [36..38), zone_id_hash at [38..70),
  zone_key_id at [70..78), epoch_id LE at [78..86)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -597,5 +597,12 @@ test = false
 doc = false
 bench = false
 
+[[bin]]
+name = "fuzz_symbol_envelope_layout"
+path = "fuzz_targets/symbol_envelope_layout.rs"
+test = false
+doc = false
+bench = false
+
 [workspace]
 members = ["."]
